_meta.description: Triodion texts for Lazarus Saturday — The Raising of Lazarus
_meta.source: OCA Department of Liturgical Music and Translations
_meta.note: This is a moveable feast — always the Saturday before Palm Sunday. Service is Vespers alone (sung on Friday evening). All stichera at Lord I Call are from the Triodion for the Raising of Lazarus.

title: Lazarus Saturday — The Raising of Lazarus

lazarus.attribution: from the Lenten Triodion, by Emperor Leo
lazarus.label: The Raising of Lazarus

hymns.text:
Wishing to see the tomb of Lazarus, O Lord,
since of Thine own will, Thou wast soon to dwell in a tomb,
Thou didst ask: "Where have you laid him?"
Learning that which was already known to Thee,
Thou didst cry to Thy beloved friend: "Lazarus, come forth!"
and he who had no breath obeyed the Lord Who gave him breath,
for Thou art the Savior of our souls.

Wishing to see the tomb of Lazarus, O Lord,
since of Thine own will, Thou wast soon to dwell in a tomb,
Thou didst ask: "Where have you laid him?"
Learning that which was already known to Thee,
Thou didst cry to Thy beloved friend: "Lazarus, come forth!"
and he who had no breath obeyed the Lord Who gave him breath,
for Thou art the Savior of our souls.

Thou hast come to the place of the burial of Lazarus, O Lord,
the tomb of a man four days dead.
Weeping for Thy friend, O Bread of Life, Thou didst raise him up.
Therefore death was bound by Thy voice,
and grave clothes were loosened by Thy hand.
The band of disciples was filled with joy.
One song of adoration was sung by all:
"Blessed art Thou, O Savior! Have mercy on us!"

Thy voice destroyed the kingdom of hell, O Lord.
Thy powerful word raised from the tomb the one who was four days dead.
Lazarus became the saving first-fruits of the world's regeneration.
All things are possible for Thee, O Lord and King of all.
Grant Thy servants cleansing and great mercy!

Thou didst come to the tomb of Lazarus, O Lord,
and didst call him by name,
wishing to assure Thy disciples of Thine own Resurrection.
Hell was vanquished!
It released Lazarus who was four days dead.
He cried out to Thee:
"Glory to Thee, O blessed Lord!"

Thou didst come to Bethany to wake Lazarus, O Lord,
accompanied by Thy disciples.
Thou didst weep for him,
but he was raised by Thine almighty power.
A man four days dead cried to Thee, O our Savior:
"Glory to Thee, O blessed Lord!"

glory.attribution: from the Lenten Triodion, by Andrew the Blind
glory.label: Glory and Now
glory.text:
We have completed the forty days that profit our souls.
Let us sing: "Rejoice, city of Bethany, home of Lazarus!
Rejoice, Mary and Martha, his sisters!
Tomorrow Christ will come and raise your dead brother to life.
Bitter and unsatisfied, hell will hear His voice.
Shaking and groaning, it will release bound Lazarus.
The assembly of Hebrews will be amazed.
They will greet Him with palms and branches.
Though their priests and elders look on Him with envy and malice,
the children shall praise Him in song:
'Blessed is He Who comes in the Name of the Lord,
the King of Israel!'"

prokeimenon1.refrain: Our help is in the Name of the Lord, / Who made heaven and earth.

verses.text: "If it had not been the Lord Who was on our side," let Israel now say.

prokeimenon2.refrain: Those who trust in the Lord are like Mount Zion, / which cannot be moved, but abides forever.

verses.text: For the scepter of wickedness shall not rest upon the land allotted to the righteous.

idiomelon.label: Idiomelon
idiomelon.text:
We have completed the forty days that profit our souls.
Now let us beg the Lover of man:
enable us to see the Holy Week of Thy Passion,
that we may glorify Thy mighty work,
Thy wonderful plan for our salvation,
as we sing with one heart and voice:
"O Lord, glory to Thee!"

martyrs.label: For the Martyrs
martyrs.text:
O martyrs of the Lord,
we beseech you to intercede with our God,
that He may forgive our many sins,
and grant our souls abundant mercy.

lazarus.label: Glory and Now
lazarus.text:
Standing by the tomb of Lazarus, O Savior,
Thou didst call to Thy friend, who was dead.
He heard Thy voice, and awoke as from sleep.
Mortality was shaken by immortality.
By Thy word the bound was unbound.
All is possible! All things serve and submit to Thee, O loving Lord.
O our Savior, glory to Thee!

lazarus.label: Troparion
lazarus.text:
By raising Lazarus from the dead before Thy Passion,
Thou didst confirm the universal resurrection, O Christ God.
Like the children with the palms of victory,
we cry out to Thee, O Vanquisher of Death:
"Hosanna in the highest!
Blessed is He that comes in the Name of the Lord."
